summary: A 1952 CIA memorandum reports on British activity regarding unidentified flying objects, describing a standing UK committee on flying saucers and an incident at a Yorkshire RAF field about ten to twelve days prior in which senior RAF officials and pilots reportedly observed a 'perfect flying saucer' during a demonstration, generating public press coverage. The memo also references a 1946 Swedish incident and a Naval film from the Tremonton incident, and a follow-up page discusses general potential dangers such as identification delays, mass hysteria, and communications overload from such reports.
